title: filter reads whole file into memory before the size cap rejects it
evidence: cli_filter reads input_path.read_text() (ocman.py:5055) before check_egress_guards enforces filter_max_bytes; the security IPD specified an st_size pre-check before reading. Local single-user tool, user-chosen file, so no DoS; efficiency/gap only.
impact: A multi-GB file is fully read into RAM before the cap triggers (wasted memory; no crash on realistic files)
recommended_action: Add input_path.stat().st_size >= filter_max_bytes pre-check before read_text (unless --force)

title: secret-scan redaction verified (positive)
evidence: scan_for_secrets returns only (kind,line); check_egress_guards error emits 'kind@Lline' deduped/sorted, never the matched value. Verified by test_filter_secret_scan_blocks asserting the value is absent.
impact: No secret leakage in the new guard's own error output

title: collision helper protects live sessions (positive/LIVE)
evidence: LIVE surface: on would-overwrite, resolve_recovery_collision runs check_opencode_process_lock (refuse if opencode running) then backs up (.bu.NNN), never deletes non-interactively. Prevents clobbering a live session's recovery file. Reviewed by reading the path, not tests.
impact: Prevents overwrite of an in-use recovery artifact; no data-loss path found

title: TUI parity behaviors (canonical name / default_out_dir / compacted-copy) not pinned by a test
evidence: test_tui_compaction_end_to_end_network_mocked proves the TUI still writes a .compacted.md and succeeds, but does not assert the new canonical YYYYMMDD-HHMM-<full_sid> name, honoring default_out_dir, or the compacted-copy parity call that the compatibility IPD required. Regression cover for those specific behaviors is absent.
impact: A future change could regress TUI/CLI naming parity or drop the compacted-copy without a test failing
recommended_action: Add assertions to the TUI test: name matches parse_recovery_name as (sid,dt,'compacted'); a non-default default_out_dir is honored; maybe_copy_compacted_to_project is invoked (mock).
next_step: Fix in S7 (Low RR - test only)

title: Migration script not discoverable from README (upgrade path)
evidence: scripts/migrate_recovery_names.py is documented in ARCHITECTURE.md + CHANGELOG but not README. A user upgrading with old-scheme recovery files on disk would not find it from the README. filter itself is only a one-line arg-table row (no usage blurb), acceptable for a secondary command.
impact: Upgrade discoverability gap for the one-shot rename migration
recommended_action: Add a short README note near the recovery-file naming tip: old files can be normalized with scripts/migrate_recovery_names.py <dir> (--dry-run first).

title: --force help text does not mention it now overrides the filter/--compact size cap
evidence: 1.1.0 wired force into check_egress_guards so --force also bypasses filter_max_bytes for filter/--compact, but the --force help still says only 'Bypass active process lock checks during deletion or cleanup.' Incomplete/slightly misleading self-documentation.
impact: User cannot learn from --help that --force overrides the egress size cap
recommended_action: Extend the --force help text to note it also overrides the filter/--compact input size cap (filter_max_bytes).
